rulings.Case Title: SCOTT DAVID ROBISON VS. CITGO PETROLEUM CORPORATION, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 21, 2012, Line 2. DEFENDANT UNITED REFINING COMPANY'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. The Court will execute the parties' stipulation and order regarding Defendant United Refining Co.'s dismissal with one change to the last sentence of paragraph 5: "If no trial date has been set at the time United is re-served, Plaintiff and Defendant United Refining shall request that the trial date not be set within six (6) months of the date United was served with the amended summons and complaint." The Court takes the motion to quash off calendar as moot. The parties can appear but no appearance is necessary because the matter is unopposed and the Court has the parties' proposed order which it can modify. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/MJM)

This matter will be heard at 9:00 am on the Discovery Calendar in Department 301. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JACQUELINE JOHNSON VS. MILENA TALIAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Milena Talian, Mission Bay Housing Ptnrs, Lp, Fillmore Marketplace, Lp, Related Management Company, Lp 'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 21, 2012, Line 6, Defendants Milena Talian, Mission Bay Housing Ptnrs, Lp, Fillmore Marketplace, Lp, Related Management Company, Lp 'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The parties are directed to further meet and confer (in person) prior to the hearing about the issues presented by the motion. The court will reserve a ruling until the hearing. Plaintiff shall immediately furnish defense counsel and the court with a contact address (not a P.O. box).

rulings.Case Title: CAESAR A CHURCHWELL DR et al VS. PARADISE MISSIONARY BAPTIST CHURCH, INC(CORPORATIO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application For Issuance Of Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 21, 2012, Line 16. PETITIONERS CAESAR CHURCHWELL, CONCERNED MEMBERS OF PARADISE MISSIONARY BAPTIST"S Notice Of Application For Issuance Of Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus. The petition for writ of mandate and declaratory relief is denied. The Court denies the motion as to all of the claims for declaratory relief because this motion is not the proper vehicle to adjudicate those causes of action. The Court denies the petition for writ of mandate. The evidence indicates that the articles of incorporation were amended to increase the board of directors from 5 to 7 individuals. The parties are to meet and confer regarding the document inspection and that inspection shall occur by September 7, 2012. Plaintiff's request for an independent accountant/inspector is denied without prejudice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)
